title: European Union Visitors Program (EUVP) 2027: Fully Funded Study Tour to Brussels for Global Professionals
summary: The European Union Visitors Program (EUVP) 2027 is now open for applications, offering a prestigious, fully funded opportunity for professionals to engage directly with European Union institutions through a tailored study visit in Brussels and Strasbourg. Established in 1974 by the European Parliament and the European Commission, the EUVP is one of the European Union’s flagship international exchange programmes. It is designed to strengthen global partnerships, enhance mutual understanding, and foster long-term collaboration between the EU and professionals worldwide. What is the European Union Visitors Program (EUVP)? The EUVP is a high-level professional exchange initiative that provides selected participants with an exclusive five-day, customized study tour of the European Union’s key institutions. Participants visit Brussels—and when possible, Strasbourg—where they engage with EU officials, policymakers, and experts. Each visit is tailored to align with the participant’s professional background, interests, and career objectives. The programme offers a unique opportunity to gain firsthand insight into how EU policies are developed, implemented, and influence global affairs. Programme Structure and Experience The EUVP is designed to be flexible and personalized, ensuring maximum value for each participant. 1. Tailor-Made Study Visits Individualized programmes based on participants’ professional interests Meetings with EU officials from the European Commission and European Parliament Exposure to EU policymaking processes and governance systems 2. Institutional Engagement Direct interaction with decision-makers and policy experts Discussions on EU priorities, policies, and international relations Opportunity to exchange ideas and perspectives with EU counterparts 3. Flexible Participation Format Visits may be individual or group-based Scheduling is coordinated based on participant availability and programme timelines What the Programme Offers The European Union Visitors Program is fully funded and provides comprehensive support to selected participants: International travel costs covered Accommodation in Brussels (and Strasbourg where applicable) Daily allowance (per diem) Health and accident insurance This ensures that participants can fully focus on their learning and engagement without financial constraints. Who Can Apply? The EUVP is open to mid-career professionals with a strong interest in European Union affairs and international relations. Eligible Candidates Include: Government officials (local, state, or federal levels) Journalists and media professionals Trade union representatives Educators and academics NGO and non-profit professionals Policy advisors and opinion leaders Basic Requirements: Age range: 25–40 years Must have completed university education or equivalent professional training Must have several years of professional experience Demonstrated interest in EU-related topics and transatlantic relations It is important to note that this programme is not open to students. Selection Criteria Applicants are evaluated based on: Professional background and relevance to EU-related topics Motivation and clarity of purpose Potential to contribute to EU-international relations Ability to apply knowledge gained to their professional field A strong statement of purpose is essential and should outline: Current responsibilities Topics of interest related to the EU Desired meetings and engagements Expected outcomes from participation Why You Should Apply The EUVP offers a rare opportunity to: Gain insider knowledge of EU institutions and policymaking Build international networks with policymakers and professionals Enhance your expertise in global governance and diplomacy Contribute to strengthening international collaboration This programme is particularly valuable for professionals seeking to deepen their engagement with European affairs and expand their global influence. Application Process Applicants must submit the following documents: Curriculum Vitae (CV) Completed Application Form Statement of Purpose Applications are reviewed by a selection committee in Brussels, jointly chaired by representatives of the European Parliament and the European Commission. For U.S. applicants, submissions should be sent to the EU Delegation in Washington, D.C. Application Timeline Application Deadline: April 1, 2026 Programme Year: 2027 (exact visit dates assigned after selection) Applicants are encouraged to apply early and ensure all documents are complete. Click HERE to learn more about the European Union Visitors Program (EUVP) 2027. Discover more global Fellowship opportunities on OFY HERE. title: Apply for CRC Research Grants 2026: First Nations Australians in Climate and Development Cooperation
summary: The 2026 Climate Resilient Communities (CRC) Research Grants Round presents a significant opportunity for organizations, researchers, and development practitioners to contribute to climate and disaster resilience efforts across the Indo-Pacific region. This initiative is funded by the Australian Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade (DFAT) and is designed to support applied research that meaningfully integrates First Nations Australians’ perspectives into development cooperation. At its core, this grant round recognizes that First Nations Australians possess deep, place-based knowledge systems, cultural practices, and leadership approaches that are invaluable in addressing climate change and disaster risks. By embedding these perspectives into development programming, the CRC program seeks to generate more inclusive, culturally appropriate, and effective resilience strategies. About the CRC Program and Research Focus The Climate Resilient Communities (CRC) program focuses on strengthening resilience to climate variability, extreme weather events, and disasters in vulnerable regions. Through this grant round, CRC aims to fund applied research that does not remain theoretical but instead produces actionable insights that can directly inform development policies and programs. The research supported under this initiative must go beyond traditional academic outputs. Instead, it should: Generate practical and usable guidance for DFAT and its partners Be grounded in real-world programming contexts Demonstrate how First Nations Australians’ knowledge systems can enhance resilience outcomes Contribute to long-term, sustainable development practices in the Indo-Pacific This emphasis on application ensures that funded projects will have tangible impacts on communities, institutions, and policy frameworks. Purpose of the Grant Round The 2026 CRC Research Grants Round is specifically designed to: Elevate First Nations Australians’ leadership in development cooperation Promote culturally informed approaches to climate and disaster resilience Strengthen partnerships between Australian First Nations organizations and Indo-Pacific stakeholders Bridge the gap between knowledge and practice through evidence-based research A key requirement is that projects must either be led by, or meaningfully partnered with, First Nations Australian organizations or entities. This ensures that Indigenous perspectives are not merely included but are central to the research design, implementation, and outcomes. Funding Streams To accommodate different types of research needs and timelines, the grant round offers two distinct funding streams: 1. Discover Stream The Discover stream is intended for comprehensive research projects that aim to build new evidence and deepen understanding. Key features include: Funding of up to $600,000 Project duration of up to 2 years Focus on in-depth research and evidence generation Suitable for complex, multi-phase studies Projects under this stream are expected to explore new insights, develop frameworks, and produce robust findings that can inform long-term strategies. 2. Accelerate Stream The Accelerate stream is designed for projects that focus on translating existing knowledge into practical applications. Key features include: Funding of up to $200,000 Project duration of up to 12 months Emphasis on rapid implementation and knowledge translation Ideal for scaling or adapting proven approaches This stream is particularly suitable for organizations that already have relevant research or experience and want to apply it quickly in real-world contexts. Eligible Organisations The CRC Research Grants Round encourages collaborative and cross-sectoral partnerships. Eligible applicants include consortia made up of organizations from: Public sector institutions Private sector entities Academic and research institutions Non-governmental organizations (NGOs) This multi-sector approach ensures that projects benefit from diverse expertise and perspectives, increasing the likelihood of impactful and sustainable outcomes. Project Requirements To qualify for funding, all proposed projects must meet the following criteria: 1. First Nations Leadership or Partnership Projects must be led by, or substantively partnered with, a First Nations Australian organization or entity. This is a non-negotiable requirement and central to the program’s objectives. 2. In-Country Collaboration Projects must involve partners located in one or more eligible Official Development Assistance (ODA) countries within the Indo-Pacific region. This ensures that research is contextually relevant and locally grounded. 3. Culturally Safe and Inclusive Practices Applicants must demonstrate: Cultural safety in engagement and implementation Inclusivity across stakeholders Respect for rights-based approaches These principles are essential to maintaining ethical standards and ensuring that research benefits all involved communities. Key Dates and Timeline Applicants should take careful note of the following important dates: Request for Proposals (RFP) Opens: 20 March 2026 RFP Closes: 10 April 2026 Deadline for Questions Submission: 6 April 2026 Final Submission Deadline: 10 April 2026 at 5:00 PM AEST It is critical to adhere strictly to these deadlines, as late submissions will not be accepted under any circumstances. Application Process Applying for the CRC Research Grants Round involves several steps: Step 1: Download Required Documents Applicants must obtain and complete the following: Request for Proposal (RFP) document Proposed Standard Grant Template document These documents contain detailed instructions, eligibility requirements, and submission guidelines. Step 2: Prepare the Proposal Applicants are required to provide comprehensive information about their proposed project, including: Research objectives and methodology Partnership structure and roles Expected outcomes and impact Budget and implementation timeline Proposals should clearly demonstrate alignment with the goals of the CRC program and highlight how First Nations perspectives will be embedded throughout the project. Step 3: Submit the Application Completed RFP forms must be submitted via email to: grants@crcsu.com.au Submission deadline: 5:00 PM Australian Eastern Standard Time (AEST) on 10 April 2026 Applicants are strongly advised to submit well before the deadline to avoid last-minute issues. Request for Information and Support Applicants who require clarification during the application process can submit questions to the CRC Secretariat through the designated RFP Question Submission Form. Key details include: Questions must be submitted no later than 6 April 2026 Responses will be provided within five (5) working days A deidentified register of questions and answers may be published publicly This transparent process ensures that all applicants have access to consistent and accurate information. Conclusion The 2026 CRC Research Grants Round represents a forward-thinking approach to development cooperation—one that values Indigenous knowledge systems, prioritizes cultural integrity, and seeks practical solutions to pressing climate challenges. By supporting research that is both inclusive and action-oriented, the CRC program is not only advancing climate and disaster resilience in the Indo-Pacific but also setting a precedent for how First Nations Australians’ perspectives can shape global development practices. Organizations with the capacity to lead or collaborate in this space are strongly encouraged to apply and contribute to this impactful initiative. Discover more similar opportunities here. The post Apply for CRC Research Grants 2026: First Nations Australians in Climate and Development Cooperation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply Now: MiRA Fellowship 2026 – Global Fellowship for Leaders Driving Systems-Level Change
summary: The MiRA Fellowship is a highly selective global program designed for individuals working on bold, systems-level solutions to complex global challenges. Offered by the MiRA Fellowship , this opportunity supports professionals who are not just addressing problems, but actively working to transform systems and create lasting, large-scale impact. Unlike traditional fellowships focused solely on academic research or short-term projects, the MiRA Fellowship is built for individuals leading ambitious initiatives in areas such as climate, social systems, development, and innovation. It provides a platform for deep reflection, collaboration, and strategic growth within a global community of changemakers. About the Opportunity The MiRA Fellowship is designed to identify and support individuals who are tackling complex systemic issues with innovative and scalable solutions. Rather than focusing on early-stage ideas, the program looks for applicants who are already engaged in meaningful work and are ready to take it to the next level. Fellows become part of a carefully curated global community, where they engage in ongoing collaboration, learning, and exchange. The program emphasizes intellectual rigor, adaptability, and openness to new perspectives, encouraging participants to challenge their assumptions and refine their approaches. A key aspect of the fellowship is its focus on systems thinking —addressing root causes rather than symptoms. This means fellows are expected to work on initiatives that aim to create structural change, whether in policy, markets, communities, or institutions. The selection process itself reflects this philosophy. It is designed to deeply understand each applicant’s work, motivations, and potential for impact, ensuring a strong fit between fellows and the community. What You’ll Gain The MiRA Fellowship offers a unique combination of support, resources, and community: One-on-one coaching tailored to your work and goals Access to a global network of highly accomplished peers and mentors Opportunities to receive micro-grants to support your initiative A collaborative environment that encourages learning and experimentation Exposure to diverse perspectives and interdisciplinary thinking Long-term support as part of an ongoing fellowship community These benefits are designed to help fellows strengthen their work, expand their impact, and navigate the challenges of driving systemic change. Eligibility The MiRA Fellowship is open to professionals from around the world who are working on ambitious, systems-changing initiatives. Ideal candidates typically: Are addressing complex, systemic problems with innovative solutions Have a strong track record and deep expertise in their field Demonstrate resilience, adaptability, and a willingness to learn Are collaborative and open to engaging with diverse perspectives Are working on projects with the potential for large-scale, lasting impact There are no strict restrictions based on nationality or region, making this a globally inclusive opportunity . Applicants may come from a wide range of sectors, including social impact, entrepreneurship, policy, research, and beyond. Why This Opportunity Matters Many of today’s global challenges—such as climate change, inequality, and economic transformation—are deeply rooted in complex systems. Addressing these issues requires more than incremental change; it requires bold ideas, sustained effort, and collaboration across disciplines and sectors. The MiRA Fellowship is important because it focuses specifically on individuals who are working at this level of complexity. By supporting systems-level thinkers and doers, the program helps accelerate solutions that can create meaningful, long-term change. It also provides a rare space for reflection and growth. Many leaders working on complex challenges operate under constant pressure, with limited opportunities to step back, reassess, and refine their strategies. This fellowship offers that space, while also connecting participants to a network that can amplify their impact. Ultimately, the MiRA Fellowship is not just about individual development—it is about strengthening the ecosystem of people working to solve the world’s most pressing problems. Application Process and Deadline The MiRA Fellowship has a rigorous and highly selective application process designed to identify exceptional individuals and ensure a strong mutual fit. Applicants are required to submit the following materials: Video Statement (2 minutes): A short video sharing a simple personal story, focusing on how you communicate and reflect Resume: A PDF outlining your professional background and experience Written Submission (Single PDF), including: Your Idea (500 words): What you are working on and its potential impact Impact Visual (250 words): A description of the world after your idea is implemented Resources Milestones (500 words): What you have, what you need, and key milestones Why You (500 words): Your motivation and unique qualifications Why Now (500 words): Why this work is urgent and timely After submission, applications are reviewed carefully. Selected candidates are typically contacted within 2–3 weeks to begin a deeper conversation about their work and potential fit within the fellowship community. This process is collaborative, allowing both the fellowship team and applicants to explore alignment and opportunities for engagement. Deadline: June 1, 2026 To learn more and apply, visit the official MiRA Fellowship website . title: Apply Now: ADIF 2026 Jobs Call to Action Challenge – Shape the Future of Job Creation in Africa
summary: The ADIF 2026 Jobs Call to Action Challenge , led by the Africa Development Impact Forum , is a unique opportunity for individuals and organizations to contribute bold, scalable solutions to one of Africa’s most urgent challenges: job creation. This initiative invites ideas, research, and practical strategies that can help generate millions of jobs across the continent and drive long-term economic growth. As part of the inaugural Africa Development Impact Forum (ADIF) 2026, this challenge goes beyond a traditional competition. It is designed as a policy innovation pipeline , connecting ideas directly to implementation by bringing together policymakers, researchers, entrepreneurs, and industry leaders. About the Opportunity Africa is facing a critical moment. By 2050, one in three young people globally will be African, and by 2030, the continent will need approximately 72.6 million new jobs to accommodate its growing population. However, despite existing solutions and successful local initiatives, many ideas fail to scale or influence policy. The ADIF Jobs Call to Action Challenge is designed to close this gap. It creates a platform where ideas are not only shared but also refined, tested, and connected to decision-makers who can implement them. Participants are invited to submit proposals that address job creation across key areas such as industrial development, trade, entrepreneurship, skills development, and the future of work. Selected participants will engage in a broader process that includes a Policy and Strategy Hackathon , collaborative sessions with experts, and opportunities to move their ideas toward real-world implementation. This initiative is part of a larger ecosystem that includes a five-month webinar series, a two-day hybrid forum in June 2026, and a post-forum accountability process to track progress and ensure that commitments translate into tangible outcomes. Why This Opportunity Matters Africa’s job creation challenge is one of the most pressing development issues of our time. High levels of unemployment and underemployment—affecting nearly 60% of young people—limit economic growth, increase inequality, and pose risks to social stability. At the same time, Africa is rich with ideas, innovation, and entrepreneurial energy. The challenge lies not in a lack of solutions, but in the gap between ideas and implementation . The ADIF initiative addresses this gap by creating a structured process that connects innovation with policy and action. By bringing together stakeholders from across sectors, it enables collaborative problem-solving and ensures that promising ideas are not lost but developed into impactful solutions. This opportunity is particularly important because it empowers individuals and organizations to actively shape the future of Africa’s economy, rather than simply observe it. What You’ll Gain This opportunity offers far more than visibility—it provides a pathway to real impact: Direct Influence on Policy – Contribute ideas that can shape national and regional job creation strategies Access to Decision-Makers – Engage with policymakers, private sector leaders, and development experts Collaboration Opportunities – Work alongside diverse stakeholders to co-develop scalable solutions Implementation Pathway – Top ideas move beyond theory into actionable programs Continental Exposure – Showcase your work on a high-level African platform Professional Growth – Gain experience in policy innovation, strategic thinking, and cross-sector collaboration Unlike many idea competitions, this challenge is structured to ensure that selected solutions are not only recognized but also supported toward implementation. Eligibility The ADIF Jobs Call to Action Challenge is open to a wide and diverse group of participants. Eligible applicants include: Policymakers and government representatives Researchers and academics Entrepreneurs and private sector professionals Youth leaders and innovators Think tanks and civil society organizations Applicants can come from across all African countries and the global diaspora , as long as their ideas focus on job creation in Africa. The challenge particularly encourages participation from individuals and organizations with practical experience, applied research, or innovative solutions that can be scaled. There are no strict academic requirements, but proposals should demonstrate feasibility, scalability, and potential for real-world impact . Application Process and Deadline To participate, applicants must submit their ideas through the official online application form. Submissions should clearly outline: The proposed solution or idea Its relevance to job creation in Africa Evidence, examples, or best practices supporting the idea Potential for scalability and implementation After submission: Applications will be reviewed and shortlisted Top 10 candidates will be invited to participate in the Policy and Strategy Hackathon in June 2026 Selected ideas will be further developed with experts and stakeholders Winning solutions will move toward implementation and impact tracking Learn more about ADIF on their official website . If you would like to apply to the Jobs Challenge Call to Action, use this form. Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. title: Impactful Twilio Social Impact Internship 2026 for Global Community Engagement
summary: The Impactful Twilio Social Impact Internship 2026 offers an exciting and hands-on opportunity for students passionate about corporate social responsibility, community engagement, and global development. Hosted by Twilio’s Social and Environmental Impact (SEI) team, this internship provides real-world experience at the intersection of technology, social impact, and communications. With applications open until April 1, 2026 (subject to change), this internship is designed for students eager to contribute meaningfully to global social impact initiatives while gaining exposure to one of the world’s leading tech organizations. Impactful Twilio Internship Experience in Global Social Impact Unlike traditional internships, the Impactful Twilio Social Impact Internship 2026 is not a passive learning experience. Interns are actively involved in designing, supporting, and scaling social impact programs that operate on a global level. Twilio is a leading communications platform that empowers businesses and developers worldwide. Through its Social and Environmental Impact team, the company drives initiatives that leverage technology to create positive change in communities across the globe. This internship places you directly within these efforts, allowing you to contribute to impactful projects that align business strategy with social good. Impactful Twilio Role and Responsibilities As a Social Impact Intern, you will play a key role in supporting Twilio’s community engagement and corporate social responsibility initiatives. The position is structured across three major focus areas: Impactful Employee Engagement and Program Coordination (50%) • Assist in organizing and executing employee volunteer programs • Support the planning and coordination of Twilio’s Global Impact Week, involving over 50 volunteer events worldwide • Develop internal communications such as newsletters, intranet content, and impact reports Impactful Community Engagement and Research (30%) • Conduct research on key sectors including education, healthcare, and crisis readiness • Identify high-impact nonprofit partnerships where Twilio’s technology can drive meaningful change • Support the development of strategies that enhance community outcomes through digital communication tools Impactful Operations and Analysis (20%) • Prepare reports, presentations, and internal documentation • Analyze trends in the public sector technology and crisis readiness landscape • Identify opportunities to improve internal systems and program efficiency This structure ensures that interns gain a well-rounded experience across strategy, execution, and analysis. Impactful Learning and Professional Development The internship offers a comprehensive learning experience that prepares students for careers in social impact, business strategy, and technology. Key benefits include: • Hands-on experience in corporate social responsibility within a global tech company • Exposure to cross-functional teams including Sales, Marketing, Product, and Operations • Mentorship and guidance from experienced professionals within the Social and Environmental Impact team • Opportunities to build professional networks across a global organization Interns will gain valuable insights into how large-scale organizations integrate social impact into their core business strategies. Impactful Work Environment at Twilio Twilio is known for its remote-first work culture, which emphasizes flexibility, inclusion, and global collaboration. Employees work from various locations while remaining connected through a strong and supportive company culture. This internship is fully remote, allowing candidates to contribute from anywhere within eligible locations while engaging with a diverse, international team. The organization also prioritizes innovation, encouraging employees and interns to think creatively, solve problems, and contribute new ideas. Impactful Compensation and Benefits The Impactful Twilio Social Impact Internship 2026 offers competitive hourly compensation based on location: • $29/hour (Colorado, Hawaii, Illinois, Maryland, Massachusetts, Minnesota, Vermont, Washington D.C.) • $31/hour (New York, New Jersey, Washington State, California outside the Bay Area) • $35/hour (San Francisco Bay Area) In addition to compensation, interns benefit from: • Flexible work arrangements • Access to professional development resources • Exposure to global impact initiatives • Opportunities to engage in volunteering and community-building efforts Impactful Candidate Profile Twilio is seeking candidates who are passionate, adaptable, and eager to contribute to meaningful change. Ideal applicants should: • Be currently pursuing a degree in Political Science, International Affairs, Business Administration, Non-Profit Management, or related fields • Have a strong interest in social impact and corporate responsibility • Demonstrate strong communication, organizational, and problem-solving skills • Be proactive, innovative, and collaborative • Show an entrepreneurial mindset and willingness to learn The company values diverse backgrounds and encourages applications from individuals with non-traditional career paths. Deadline for Applications The deadline to apply for the Impactful Twilio Social Impact Internship 2026 is April 1, 2026, though applications may close earlier depending on business needs. Learn More About This Opportunity To learn more about the Impactful Twilio Social Impact Internship 2026 and apply, click here : Conclusion The Impactful Twilio Social Impact Internship 2026 is an exceptional opportunity for students to gain real-world experience in global social impact while working within a leading technology company. By combining innovation, community engagement, and corporate responsibility, this internship equips participants with the skills and insights needed to drive meaningful change. For more internship opportunities on OFY Opportunities click here The post Impactful Twilio Social Impact Internship 2026 for Global Community Engagement appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNIDO Internship Programme: Fully Explained (Eligibility, Benefits, Application Process)
summary: The UNIDO Internship Programme offers an exceptional opportunity for students and recent graduates to gain hands-on experience in international development, industrial innovation, and sustainable economic growth . If you are passionate about contributing to inclusive and sustainable industrial development , this paid internship programme provides a direct entry point to the United Nations system while building valuable professional and cross-cultural skills. This guide covers the full details of the programme, including eligibility requirements, benefits, responsibilities, and the application process. About UNIDO The United Nations Industrial Development Organization (UNIDO) works to promote inclusive and sustainable industrial development (ISID) . Through its programmes and partnerships, UNIDO supports countries in fostering industrial innovation, environmental sustainability, digital transformation, job creation, and economic growth. An internship at UNIDO offers exposure to these global initiatives while gaining real-world professional experience. Who Can Apply for the UNIDO Internship Programme? To be eligible, candidates must meet one of the following criteria: Currently enrolled in the final year of a Bachelor’s degree programme Currently enrolled in a Master’s degree programme (or higher) Graduated with a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree within the last 12 months Additional requirements: Fluency in the working language of the duty station Must be a national of a UNIDO Member States Where UNIDO Internships Take Place Internships are offered at: UNIDO Headquarters in Vienna, Austria UNIDO field offices worldwide Opportunities are posted regularly and vary by department, region, and focus area. Duration and Start Dates Internship duration: 3 to 6 months Sponsored internships: up to 12 months Start date: First working day of each month UNIDO Internship Stipend and Financial Support Beginning September 2025 , UNIDO interns receive a monthly stipend to support living expenses. Additional details: Area Responsibility Monthly internship stipend Provided starting September 2025 Travel, visa, accommodation, insurance, personal costs Covered by intern Visa support letter Provided by UNIDO Official UN travel on duty Not authorized for interns Note: This internship does not guarantee employment , but experience gained is highly valued across UN agencies and international development organizations. Many UN entities count internship experience toward minimum professional requirements for entry-level roles. Key Candidate Qualities UNIDO seeks motivated, ethical, and mission-driven candidates. Ideal applicants demonstrate: Integrity and professionalism Respect for diversity and cultural sensitivity Teamwork and collaborative skills Curiosity and willingness to innovate Strong communication and research abilities Selection Process All applications are reviewed Only shortlisted candidates are contacted for interviews or assessments Applications remain in UNIDO’s database for up to one year for future opportunities Due to high competition, it is recommended to tailor your application to the specific internship posting. How to Apply for the UNIDO Internship Programme Internships are advertised throughout the year. To apply: Visit the UNIDO Careers Portal . Search for available internship postings Review eligibility and required qualifications Submit your application through the online system Tip: Prepare a strong resume and motivation statement highlighting interest in industrial development, sustainability, global cooperation, innovation, and international organizations . Why Choose the UNIDO Internship Programme? A UNIDO internship is valuable for those pursuing careers in: International development Sustainable industrial transformation Government policy and economic development Climate action and green innovation Programme management and research Youth and community development Industrial engineering, economics, or business fields This experience enhances professional credibility and networking opportunities within the UN system and beyond. 👉 Explore Internship Opportunities . Title: Intern Sustainable Investment and Responsible Business (2 positions) Location: Vienna, Austria Category: Internship Programme Grade: Intern Application Deadline: 03 April 2026 👉 Apply today at careers.unido.org Click on the video to find out what it is like to be an intern at UNIDO! Tips for a Strong Application Customize your cover letter : Address why you’re interested in the specific internship and how your background aligns with the role. Show relevant experience : Use coursework, projects, volunteer work, or past internships to demonstrate skills in compliance, communications, policy, or related areas. Be clear about availability : State when you can start and for how long (3-6 months). Polish your documents : Make sure your resume/CV, transcripts, and any samples are updated and error-free. Mission Organizational Background UNIDO is the UN’s specialized agency for industrial development , working to: Promote poverty reduction , Support inclusive globalization , Ensure environmental sustainability . Its mission is institutionalized via the Lima Declaration (2013) and the Abu Dhabi Declaration (2019) . UNIDO’s work is central to achieving SDG-9 (“Industry, Innovation, Infrastructure”) and intersects with all SDGs through its strategic priorities: Creating shared prosperity Advancing economic competitiveness Safeguarding the environment Strengthening knowledge and institutions For full details of each internship, eligibility, and to submit your application, use the Apply Here link above. Final Thoughts The UNIDO Internship Programme is a competitive and prestigious opportunity for students and graduates seeking to build a global career in sustainable development and industrial innovation. With structured learning, professional exposure, and the prestige of a UN system placement, it serves as a strong stepping stone toward future roles in multilateral institutions and the development sector. To view current openings and apply, visit the official UNIDO Careers Portal . The post UNIDO Internship Programme: Fully Explained (Eligibility, Benefits, Application Process) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: McCain Institute Junior Fellowships 2026 (Paid, USA): Eligibility, Benefits, and Application Guide
summary: The McCain Institute Junior Fellowship Program is a paid, trimester-based fellowship for undergraduate and graduate students in the United States interested in democracy, human rights, and international development . Key Details Location: United States Program Type: Junior Fellowship (Trimester-based) Funding: Stipend provided (paid opportunity) Eligibility: Undergraduate and graduate students enrolled in U.S. institutions Focus Areas: Democracy, human rights, foreign policy, leadership About the McCain Institute Junior Fellowship The McCain Institute Junior Fellowship is designed to encourage students to pursue careers in democracy, human rights, and global leadership . The program provides direct exposure to policy work and program implementation through collaboration with experts and institutional leadership. Junior Fellows work closely with teams responsible for designing, implementing, and evaluating programs that address critical global challenges. Fellowship Benefits Participants in the program will: Receive a stipend (paid fellowship) Gain hands-on experience in program design and management Collaborate with subject matter experts and senior leaders Build expertise in foreign policy and international development Expand professional networks across relevant sectors Develop skills that support long-term career growth Roles and Responsibilities Junior Fellows are expected to: Contribute to the development and implementation of programs Support research, analysis, and program evaluation activities Engage with stakeholders across policy and development sectors Strengthen their knowledge in democracy and human rights issues Participate in team-based and cross-functional projects Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Be currently enrolled in an undergraduate or graduate academic program in the United States Demonstrate a strong commitment to the mission and values of the McCain Institute Be able to successfully complete a background check Application Link: https://app.joinhandshake.com/e/108203/jobs More Information: https://www.mccaininstitute.org/programs/leadership-programs/junior-fellowships/ Why This Fellowship Matters The program reflects the Institute’s commitment to advancing a vision of human progress through leadership, realism, and global engagement . It equips emerging professionals with practical skills and exposure needed to navigate complex international challenges. How to Apply Submit your application via: https://app.joinhandshake.com/e/108203/jobs Review full program details at: https://www.mccaininstitute.org/programs/leadership-programs/junior-fellowships/ The post McCain Institute Junior Fellowships 2026 (Paid, USA): Eligibility, Benefits, and Application Guide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: SDGs Youth Public Policy Innovation Challenge 2026 (Fully Funded): Win Global Recognition & Present in China
summary: The SDGs Youth Public Policy Innovation Challenge 2026 is now open for applications, inviting young innovators, researchers, and changemakers from around the world to develop impactful policy solutions focused on protecting cultural diversity . Organized by Renmin University of China in collaboration with leading global institutions, this fully funded international competition empowers youth to contribute directly to the 2030 Sustainable Development Agenda through research, innovation, and AI-driven policy development. 🌐 About the Program The SDGs Youth Public Policy Innovation Challenge is a prestigious global initiative that brings together youth from diverse academic and professional backgrounds to address real-world policy challenges. Participants will design innovative, research-based policy proposals aimed at safeguarding and promoting cultural diversity—an essential pillar for sustainable development, social cohesion, and global peace. Finalists will be invited to Beijing and Suzhou, China , where they will present their ideas, engage in academic exchanges, and participate in field research activities. 🎯 Key Benefits of the Challenge Participating in this global competition comes with numerous academic, professional, and networking advantages: Fully Funded Participation : Selected finalists receive financial support covering airfare, accommodation, and local logistics Global Exposure : Present your ideas on an international platform to policymakers and experts Expert Mentorship : Gain guidance from leaders in public policy, AI, and cultural governance Career Opportunities : Top participants may be recommended for United Nations-related programs and internships Awards Recognition : Win prestigious awards and funding support for your research Publication Opportunities : Outstanding proposals may be considered for academic publication and policy implementation Networking : Connect with global youth leaders, scholars, and institutions 🧠 Theme: Protecting Cultural Diversity The 2026 edition focuses on “Protecting Cultural Diversity” , emphasizing the importance of preserving traditions, languages, and cultural expressions in a rapidly globalizing world. Participants are encouraged to explore innovative solutions addressing: Cultural preservation in the digital age Policy frameworks for inclusive societies AI and technology in safeguarding heritage Combating cultural homogenization Promoting intercultural dialogue and mutual understanding 👥 Who Can Apply? The competition is open to: Youth aged 18–35 years Individuals or teams of up to 5 members Students, researchers, and young professionals from all disciplines Applicants from any country worldwide 📍 Program Location Beijing Suzhou, China Finalists will attend the in-person competition and activities in China 📅 Important Dates Application Opens : March 23, 2026 Proposal Submission Deadline : April 10, 2026 (11:59 PM Beijing Time) Evaluation Period : April – June 2026 Finalists Announcement : June 2026 Final Competition (China) : July 19 – 25, 2026 📝 How to Apply Applicants must submit a detailed policy proposal aligned with the theme via the official submission platform. Guidelines for the sumbission form: Refer to the official program and proposal documents Important: Only one team representative should submit the application on behalf of the team. APPLY HERE. title: Investments Facilitation Officer at the Commonwealth Secretariat (Apply by 10 April 2026)
summary: The Commonwealth Secretariat is offering an exceptional career opportunity for experienced professionals in finance, trade, and investment. The role of Investments Facilitation Officer sits at the heart of global economic development—helping countries unlock large-scale investments and drive sustainable growth. If you are passionate about international development, project finance, and shaping impactful investment pipelines across multiple countries, this role could be your next big move. 🚀 About the Opportunity The Investments Facilitation Officer will join the Economic Development, Trade and Investment Directorate (EDTI) , contributing to initiatives that strengthen economic resilience across Commonwealth nations. A key focus of this role is supporting the Commonwealth Connectivity Agenda (CCA) —a strategic program aiming to boost intra-Commonwealth trade and investment to $2 trillion by 2030 . You will work at the intersection of: Investment policy Project preparation Capital mobilisation Transforming early-stage concepts into bankable, investment-ready projects that attract private and blended finance. 💼 Key Responsibilities 1. Project Development Pipeline Building Identify priority sectors aligned with national and Secretariat strategies Develop a pipeline of large-scale, investment-ready projects Conduct feasibility and viability assessments 2. Financial Structuring Modelling Design capital structures (equity, debt, concessional finance) Lead financial modelling and investment case development Prepare investment memoranda and pitch materials 3. Investor Engagement Collaborate with development finance institutions and private investors Facilitate investor roundtables and partnerships Support negotiations toward financial close 4. Policy Advisory Capacity Building Advise governments on improving investment readiness Deliver workshops and technical training Develop toolkits and policy guidance 5. Programme Management Track project milestones and outcomes Contribute to reporting, strategy, and programme design 🎓 Eligibility Criteria To qualify, applicants must have: A Master’s degree in Finance, Economics, Trade, Business Administration, or a related field At least 7 years of relevant experience in: Project finance Development finance Trade and investment Multilateral institutions Strong expertise in: Financial modelling Project structuring Experience working with governments and private sector stakeholders Bonus Advantage: Experience in regional connectivity initiatives or infrastructure-related sectors. 🌐 About the Commonwealth The Commonwealth of Nations is a voluntary association of 56 countries , representing 2.5 billion people worldwide. It promotes shared values including: Sustainable development Democracy and governance Economic prosperity Peace and inclusion Through its Secretariat, it provides technical assistance, policy support, and capacity building to member states. 📅 Application Deadline 10 April 2026 at 5:00 PM (BST) Late applications are typically not accepted, so early submission is strongly encouraged. 📝 Application Process Applicants must submit: A CV A Cover Letter clearly addressing the key criteria with relevant examples Make sure your application demonstrates: Impact-driven work Technical expertise Experience in international or multi-country environments 🌟 Why You Should Apply This role offers: A chance to work on high-impact global projects Exposure to international finance and policy-making Collaboration with governments, investors, and global institutions The opportunity to shape sustainable economic growth across diverse countries APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more job opportunities, click here . title: Global Changemaker Academy for Parliamentarians (G-CAP) 2026 in Mongolia: Fully Sponsored Leadership Programme on Climate Action and Land Restoration
summary: The Global Changemaker Academy for Parliamentarians (G-CAP) 2026 is now open for applications, offering a prestigious and fully sponsored leadership opportunity for parliamentarians worldwide. Scheduled to take place from 24–28 August 2026 in Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia , this exclusive five-day academy is designed to empower policymakers with the knowledge, skills, and global networks needed to advance sustainable land management and climate action. Organized by the G20 Global Land Initiative and hosted at the United Nations Convention to Combat Desertification (UNCCD), in collaboration with the United Nations System Staff College (UNSSC), the programme brings together a select group of 30 parliamentarians from across the globe to engage in high-level dialogue, training, and policy development. About the Global Changemaker Academy for Parliamentarians (G-CAP) The Global Changemaker Academy for Parliamentarians is an annual leadership initiative focused on strengthening the capacity of policymakers to address one of the most pressing global challenges—land degradation. Land restoration is a critical pillar of sustainable development. It directly contributes to: Food security Climate resilience Biodiversity conservation Sustainable economic development As lawmakers, parliamentarians play a crucial role in shaping policies, legislation, and governance frameworks that influence land use and environmental sustainability. G-CAP provides a unique platform for these leaders to collaborate, learn, and drive impactful change. Programme Highlights and Learning Components The G-CAP 2026 programme offers a rich, immersive learning experience through multiple components: 1. Masterclasses and Global Dialogues Participants will engage with leading global experts, senior UN officials, and policymakers on key topics such as: Sustainable land governance Climate policy integration Restoration strategies and implementation Global environmental frameworks 2. Immersive Field Visits Experience real-world examples of land restoration and eco-social transformation Participate in site visits, including initiatives such as mine restoration Observe the impact of policy and innovation on sustainable land use 3. Legislative Workshops Develop practical legislative approaches for land restoration Explore cross-sectoral policy integration Strengthen governance strategies at national and international levels 4. Communication and Advocacy Labs Build skills in public communication and advocacy Learn how to craft compelling narratives around sustainability Enhance influence in policy discussions and public engagement What the Programme Offers Selected participants will benefit from: Full sponsorship covering participation in the academy Access to high-level global policy discussions Opportunities to engage in the UNCCD COP17 events and dialogues Networking with international parliamentarians and experts Enhanced leadership capacity in climate and environmental governance Eligibility Criteria The programme is specifically designed for: National Members of Parliament from around the world Policymakers actively engaged in land management, climate action, or environmental governance Leaders with a demonstrated interest or experience in sustainable development and land restoration Applicants are required to provide details about their work and contributions to land conservation and management. Programme Location and Context The 2026 edition of G-CAP will be held in Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia , alongside the 17th session of the Conference of the Parties (COP17) to the UNCCD . This strategic alignment allows participants to: Engage directly with global environmental negotiations Participate in COP sessions and side events Contribute to international policy discussions on desertification and land restoration Application Timeline Programme Dates: August 24–28, 2026 Application Status: Currently Open Deadline: Not explicitly specified (early application strongly recommended) How to Apply Interested parliamentarians are required to submit their application through the Official Application form by providing details about their background and work in land restoration and sustainability. For inquiries, applicants may contact: Email: gcap@unssc.org Why You Should Apply The G-CAP 2026 programme is a rare opportunity for parliamentarians to: Gain advanced knowledge in sustainable land management Strengthen legislative and policy-making capabilities Engage with global leaders and institutions Influence national and international climate agendas Contribute to sustainable development and environmental resilience This programme is particularly valuable for policymakers seeking to drive impactful legislative change and champion environmental sustainability in their countries. Learn more about the Global Changemaker Academy for Parliamentarians (G-CAP) 2026 HERE. Click HERE to discover more global Fellowship opportunities on OFY.
